cards.goal: Complete Day 1 with steady, consistent action.

paragraphs: Isometric holds activate the superficial muscular aponeurotic system (SMAS) — the same muscle layer that a facelift targets. Consistent daily activation signals fibroblasts to produce more collagen.

cards.title: 20-Minute Walk
cards.subtitle: Wakes up circulation before facial exercises
instructions: Walk at a comfortable pace for 20 minutes.
Keep your posture upright, shoulders relaxed.
Breathe naturally through the nose if possible.
cards.purpose: Improves blood circulation, supports cellular energy production, and reduces physical tension before facial work.

cards.name: Cheek Lift Hold
steps: Sit comfortably with your back straight.
Smile as wide as possible.
Place fingertips lightly on top of cheeks.
Lift cheek muscles upward toward the eyes.
Hold firmly.
cards.scienceNote: Trains the SMAS layer — the same muscle layer facelift surgery targets — through consistent isometric activation.

cards.name: Jawline Pucker Hold
steps: Sit or stand with your back straight.
Tilt your head slightly upward toward the ceiling.
Pucker your lips as if kissing the ceiling.
Feel the stretch under the jaw and neck.
Hold firmly.
cards.scienceNote: Trains the SMAS layer — the same muscle layer facelift surgery targets — through consistent isometric activation.

cards.title: Calm Session
cards.subtitle: Nervous system reset
steps: Open the app and tap the Calm button.
Complete today's guided calm session.
If offline: sit quietly and take 20 slow breaths — inhale for 4 counts, exhale for 6 counts.
benefits: Lowers cortisol — which directly slows skin aging
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body feels steadier.

cards.title: Sleep Before 11 PM
cards.subtitle: Growth hormone releases in deep sleep before midnight
instructions: Aim to sleep before 11 PM tonight.
Turn off screens 30 minutes before bed.
Allow your body and mind to relax.
Maintain a consistent sleep time.
cards.purpose: Growth hormone — the body's main skin repair hormone — is released primarily in deep sleep before midnight. Consistent sleep timing also supports female hormone balance.
